channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: FCC approves merger of local television owners Nexstar and Tegna as two lawsuits seek to block it
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission on Thursday said it had approved the merger of local television giants Nexstar Media Group and rival Tegna, the same day that two lawsuits trying to block the deal were announced.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Communications Commission on Thursday said it had approved the merger of local television giants Nexstar Media Group and rival Tegna, the same day that two lawsuits trying to block the deal were announced.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Planned Parenthood chapter settles with EEOC over treatment of white employees
item.description: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission says Planned Parenthood of Illinois will pay $500,000 to end an investigation that found the organization's DEI practices violated federal civil rights laws
item.content:encoded:
The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission says Planned Parenthood of Illinois will pay $500,000 to end an investigation that found the organization's DEI practices violated federal civil rights laws

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: What levers can the U.S. pull to control gas prices?
item.description: The global oil trade is remarkably flexible. But key solutions that should be able to address the current oil crisis – like reserves, alternate routes, and boosts in production — are constrained.
item.content:encoded: The global oil trade is remarkably flexible. But key solutions that should be able to address the current oil crisis – like reserves, alternate routes, and boosts in production — are constrained.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: This tax season, there's a new deduction for interest on car loans
item.description: Taxpayers who purchased a new vehicle in 2025 may qualify for a new deduction on their taxes — even if they're not itemizing. But not everyone is eligible.
item.content:encoded:
Taxpayers who purchased a new vehicle in 2025 may qualify for a new deduction on their taxes — even if they're not itemizing. But not everyone is eligible.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: It's called 'private credit' — and it could lead to big trouble on Wall Street
item.description: The risky lending business has been booming, but now its problems are becoming increasingly visible on Wall Street and beyond.
item.content:encoded:
The risky lending business has been booming, but now its problems are becoming increasingly visible on Wall Street and beyond.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: The Pentagon wants an extra $200 billion for the Iran war and beyond
item.description: The Trump administration says it is seeking a huge budget to refill and expand its munitions.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration says it is seeking a huge budget to refill and expand its munitions.

(Image credit: Oliver Contreras)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Trump temporarily waives the Jones Act to try to lower gasoline prices. Will it work?
item.description: The Jones Act restricts which ships can carry goods between U.S. ports. Experts say temporarily lifting the act will do little to affect gas prices.
item.content:encoded:
The Jones Act restricts which ships can carry goods between U.S. ports. Experts say temporarily lifting the act will do little to affect gas prices.

(Image credit: Damian Dovarganes)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: The Fed holds interest rates steady as the economy faces deep uncertainty
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady as it faces inflationary pressure from the war with Iran — and a weakening labor market.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady as it faces inflationary pressure from the war with Iran — and a weakening labor market.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Arizona AG files criminal charges against Kalshi over 'illegal gambling'
item.description: Arizona is the first state to allege the prediction market company has committed criminal violations, accusing it of running an unlicensed gambling operation.
item.content:encoded:
Arizona is the first state to allege the prediction market company has committed criminal violations, accusing it of running an unlicensed gambling operation.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Judge orders 1,000 Voice of America staffers back to work in rebuke to Kari Lake
item.description: A federal judge has ordered more than a thousand Voice of America staffers back to work by Monday. It's a major defeat for the Trump administration's effort to cut the news outlet to the bones.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge has ordered more than a thousand Voice of America staffers back to work by Monday. It's a major defeat for the Trump administration's effort to cut the news outlet to the bones.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik
